column1: Pandazilla: Thank you for the game
C[satorian: sorry for the delay. been occupied this weekend.
jimhackermp: greetings!!!! thanks for accepting my request for a game :)
jimhackermp: (don't know how much English you speak, I only took one semester of German and that was a LONG time ago lol)
satorian: no problem there. i'm less worried about my english than i am about my go skills, which are basically non-existent beyond reading an introductory book
C[jimhackermp: (don't know how much English you speak, I only took one semester of German and that was a LONG time ago lol)
satorian: no problem there. i'm less worried about my english than i am about my go skills, which are basically non-existent beyond reading an introductory book

C[jimhackermp: which introductory book do you have if I may ask? I've got ""Learn to Play Go"" (Janice Kim and Jeong Soo-hyun) and ""Go: A complete introduction to the game"" (Cho Chikun author) but am having a bit of trouble getting through them even though they're supposed to be ""elementary"".  Better I just play with other people and experiment with the game that way.
satorian: i've got the series by janice kim as well. read the first book and browsed through the second one. sadly my motivation and time to play come and go like the tides...
satorian: how did you stumble upon the game?
C[jimhackermp: Saw it on my university's website of student organizations, our Go Club.
jimhackermp: But, I think I saw it being played there first, not sure.
C[satorian: ah, ok. what did you major in at university?

jimhackermp: Originally, political science, then changed it to something called ""business technology administration"" because I wanted to not be under- or unemployed when I graduated...(working @ McDonald's or something in other words lol)
C[jimhackermp: I concentrated the classes I took for political science in International Relations sort of topics...
jimhackermp: are you in college? (at university I mean)
C[satorian: not anymore. studied law for a while, then dropped out, interned and jobbed for a while. now, at 32, i've been running an arthouse cinema with two friends of mine for the past three years
C[satorian: so where are you working at?
C[jimhackermp: arthouse cinema? Also a dropout, here, from UMBC (The University of Maryland Baltimore County)
jimhackermp: so not working anywhere right now :(
C[satorian: what happened? didn't enjoy it anymore?
satorian: this is the site of our cinema: http://www.roxydortmund.de/

jimhackermp: can't read much of it , lol, but it looks fascinating. A ""real cinema"" sort of theater, where you play the movies? They are dying in the United States what with the advent of the Internet and movies on demand on cable & satellite, but some people still like them.

jimhackermp: well, I have something called ADHD (not a ""mental"" disease, but a sort of.....dunno how to explain it) and it had affected my concentration to the point where I could not study enough hours in the day to keep up.
jimhackermp: I had been going part time for some years, and it was within the last year or two that the ADHD got worse (the medications less effective)
C[satorian: that's too bad about your ADHD
satorian: hope you'll get on top of it
satorian: and, yes, it's a classic movie theater. difficult to keep running nowadays, but it's satisfying work if you love movies and the cinema experience

jimhackermp: thanks. In the future, I am sure, they'll invent better/more effective drugs. I can still read *some* just not always when I really want to.  Getting through large books, or even instructional books, is more difficult than fo rmost people
C[jimhackermp: oh hellyeah, I LOVE ""real"" movies theatres

C[jimhackermp: so anyway, because it's hard for me to read instructional books at times, including the ones on Go, i have to learn by ""experimenting"" and ""consulting"" with other players, who are of about the same ability level as myself
C[satorian: that sounds like you would benefit from playing live games, as you'd be able to grind through more experience quicker
jimhackermp: yes, i was playing with the other guy I'm playing with right now...and we were ""live"" for a while at first, and I seemed to do rather well live...
C[satorian: i was rather thinking of the learning process as a whole, not the performane in a singular specific game
jimhackermp: Right I understand.  I had at one point frequented the IGS server for live play (about a dozen games live) and it was indeed helpful for the learning process. :)
jimhackermp: But there is a Baltimore Go Club to play in person, though there was a certain amount of impatience in their dealings with me, lol
C[jimhackermp: do you normally play black or white?
satorian: i've barely played anything so far. this is my second game
C[jimhackermp: Oh wow...I've only done it in person once or twice.  Online, a bunch of times...
C[satorian: would you mind pausing the game for a while? i'm going to nyc for two weeks, and i don't think i'll have much opportunity to put in my moves
jimhackermp: email me when you need it unpaused...
C[jimhackermp: are you ready yet? or need it to continue to remain paused?
C[jimhackermp: oh gosh im sorry i did not get the message, until now.  I'll make my move.
C[Pandazilla: Hello! Thank you for the game!
joellercoaster: Likewise - nice to meet you!
C[Pandazilla: Hello! Thank you for the game!
joellercoaster: Likewise - nice to meet you!
C[Pandazilla: First contested corner and I'm already thinking: ""I wish I had studied basic 3-4 joseki,""
C[joellercoaster: If it helps, I only know one joseki and it's about 4-4 :)
C[joellercoaster: (amusingly as it turns out, it is this one :D)

Pandazilla: It's one of the two most basic 4-4 josekis isn't it? I've looked at Kogo's a few times and seen a few in videos, so i recognize some josekis when i see them being played out, but i can rarely recall anything in my own play
C[joellercoaster: I'm probably the wrong person to ask about joseki... when I was 25k or so, Bill Spight said something like ""For the moment, forget about joeski"". So I did.
joellercoaster: And it's gotten me this far. I am starting to at least recognise patterns though.

joellercoaster: Also I watched a couple of Nick Sibicky's videos (one about 'must-know joeski' or something and one about nine responses to the low approach to the 4-4 stone but kind of didn't take them in.
C[Pandazilla: I've seen some of this videos as well, and rarely remember anything specific. I hope it just instills some kind of sense for flow and shape of a game.

C[joellercoaster: Interestingly, I got accused of 'joseki autopilot' in this game review here. So I guess I have picked up more ingrained joseki stuff than I thought (just enough to use it in the wrong place)! http://www.lifein19x19.com/forum/viewtopic.php?p=176550#p176550
C[Pandazilla: Interesting. Patterns, patterns, patterns and repeated exposure.
C[joellercoaster: I always knew in the abstract how much of a pattern repetition game Go was... now I'm starting to really *feel* how much that is true, and how huge the amount of stuff to know is.
joellercoaster: Time to hit the tsumego :)
C[Pandazilla: Yeah, just too bad I feel like a huge idiot on those supposedly ""easy"" and ""basic"" tsumegos i get constantly wrong

joellercoaster: If it helps, I am terrible at them. I think I've gotten as far as I have by having a feeling for space and being OK at contact fights (like this one). Now I'm coming up against players who understand life and death (and shape) properly and I'm getting pounded.
joellercoaster: So having made this move, I am going to go and do a chapter of Graded Go Problems For Beginners :P
C[Pandazilla: Oh man, I didn't read this out at all.
Pandazilla: I think I might be losing focus here. May we could try turning this into a live game at some point.

C[joellercoaster: For sure! I'm in London, so my timezone is presently GMT right now. I work pretty late, so mornings could work, or we could keep the game alive until the weekend and try and find time then?
C[Pandazilla: I'm usually at work from late afternoon on, so mornings should work for me, although my time is quite limited at the moment, probably until the weekend next week
C[joellercoaster: We'll figure something out. Mornings are OK for me too on Tuesdays to Thursdays, I can get up early and play?
C[Pandazilla: Hmm, I feel like this game isn't going well for me, and I'm getting into situations more complicated than I can handle
Pandazilla: the week after next perhaps, if this isn't wrapped up by then?

joellercoaster: One of the good things about corresponsence though is that you can use Analyze Mode to work it through... though I admit our current fight is a bit ridiculous and I'm not following it through particularly either.
C[joellercoaster: I've paused the game so you don't time out - let me know if you want to abandon it and play a different, realtime game some other time. Otherwise you can unpause when you're ready.
Pandazilla: Oh man, thank you for doing this. I got swamped at work suddenly (I run a cinema and we had it rented out in addition to our normal programming)
Pandazilla: Althoug I don't think I can really salvage anything here...
C[Pandazilla: Oh, I'm sorry. I actually just came here to resign. Currently not at the right place to continue correspondence games. Thank you for the opportunity though!
GN[Friendly social game to play, learn, chat]
C[llblumire: Þjaml upi fpt Þje aŋe
llblumire: thank you for the game
Satorian: Thank you for the game!
Satorian: Quite a bit to learn from this one for me.
